Details: One study reported aggregate results of a program in Florida that installed more than 800 point of entry GAC filters. With media replacement every six months, only 89 of the filters at 53 installations experienced breakthrough (defined as detectable ethylene dibromide above an analytical limit of 0.02 ug/L). Many of these results were breakthrough in the lead filter of a series; only 53 breakthroughs at 40 installations reached the final filter. Thus, the majority of installations studied achieved removals of up to greater than 99 percent [2490]. A study of a small (1 cubic foot) commercial GAC filter installed in the pump house of a private well showed similarly high removals. For 11 months, the filter removed ethylene dibromide to below an analytical limit of 0.01 ug/L (greater than 99 percent removal). Although ethylene dibromide was detected after 12 months, removal at that time was still greater than 98 percent [2494].
